 {"id":"frankenredis-42df","title":"Add redis-cli compatibility smoke test script","description":"# redis-cli Compatibility Smoke Test

## What
A shell script that starts FrankenRedis, runs redis-cli commands against it, and verifies outputs. This is the ultimate "drop-in replacement" proof.

## Why
If redis-cli cannot talk to FrankenRedis, it is not a drop-in replacement regardless of what internal tests say.

## Implementation
1. scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh
2. Start frankenredis on a random port
3. Run redis-cli commands:
   - PING → PONG
   - SET foo bar → OK
   - GET foo → bar
   - INFO server → contains "redis_version"
   - DBSIZE → (integer) 1
   - CONFIG GET maxmemory → valid response
   - MULTI/EXEC transaction
   - EVAL "return redis.call(\"GET\",KEYS[1])" 1 foo → bar
4. Check exit code of each command
5. Clean up

## Depends On
- redis-cli must be installed (check and skip if not available)

## Files
- scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh (new)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.374165839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:07.348902310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:07.348633631Z","close_reason":"Added scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh: 25 tests covering PING, SET/GET, APPEND, EXISTS, DEL, INCR/INCRBY, RPUSH/LLEN/LPOP, HSET/HGET, SADD/SISMEMBER/SCARD, INFO, DBSIZE, EVAL, CONFIG GET. All 25/25 pass using legacy redis-cli against FrankenRedis.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-e1e9","title":"Add persistence restart integration test (write → stop → restart → verify)","description":"# Persistence Restart Test

## What
An integration test that writes data to FrankenRedis with persistence enabled, gracefully stops the server, restarts it, and verifies all data survived. Tests BOTH AOF and RDB paths.

## Why
AOF round-trip is tested at the unit level (8 tests in aof_roundtrip_test.rs) but never through the full server lifecycle: TCP write → persist → shutdown → startup → reload → TCP read. The RDB path has encode/decode unit tests but no server-lifecycle test.

## Implementation

### Test A: AOF Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --aof /tmp/test_aof_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys with values, LPUSH list1 with 10 elements, HSET hash1 with 5 fields, ZADD zset1 with 5 members, XADD stream1 with 3 entries, XGROUP CREATE on stream1
3. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE (graceful exit without extra save — AOF already captured writes)
4. Restart frankenredis with same --aof path
5. Via TCP: verify all 50 keys, list contents, hash fields, zset scores, stream entries, consumer group exists
6. Verify DBSIZE matches expected count

### Test B: RDB Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --rdb /tmp/test_rdb_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys, same data structures as above
3. Send SAVE (force RDB snapshot)
4. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE
5. Restart frankenredis with same --rdb path
6. Via TCP: verify all data survived
7. Verify stream consumer groups survived (tests the new RDB consumer group persistence)

### Logging
- Log each write operation with key name
- Log verification result for each key (PASS/FAIL)
- Log file sizes (AOF bytes, RDB bytes) after write phase
- Log reload time on restart

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/persistence_restart_e2e.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.255131142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:07.886573595Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:07.886287262Z","close_reason":"Added tcp_aof_restart_preserves_all_data and tcp_rdb_restart_preserves_all_data E2E tests. Also added Runtime::load_rdb() and RDB loading on server startup. All 11 fr-server tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-e3hr","title":"Implement LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking","description":"## What
LATENCY HISTOGRAM currently returns an empty array (stub). Redis returns cumulative distribution
data for per-command latencies when enabled.

## Location
crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12152-12158

## Current behavior
```rust
// LATENCY HISTOGRAM [command ...]
// Returns cumulative distribution of latencies for commands.
// Currently returns an empty array as per-command latency histograms
// are not yet tracked. Full implementation would return map-like data.
// TODO: Implement full histogram tracking per-command.
Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![])))
```

## Design
1. Add HdrHistogram or similar per-command latency tracking to Store or Runtime
2. Record command execution times in dispatch_argv return path
3. Return histogram bucket data in Redis-compatible format

## Acceptance criteria
- LATENCY HISTOGRAM returns actual per-command latency distributions
- Compatible with Redis LATENCY HISTOGRAM output format
- Minimal overhead when not actively querying histograms","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:03:01.317581137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.894705874Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.891747186Z","close_reason":"Implemented LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking with power-of-2 microsecond buckets, integrated latency recording at all command dispatch points","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-gess","title":"Record initial performance baselines and check into baselines/ directory","description":"# Initial Baselines

## What
Run the benchmark harness against both FrankenRedis and legacy Redis, record the results, and check them into baselines/.

## Why
The baselines/ directory is empty. Without baselines, there is no way to detect performance regressions or prove improvements. The README claims "profile-proven performance" which requires actual numbers.

## Implementation
1. Build frankenredis in release mode: `cargo build --release -p fr-server`
2. Start frankenredis on port 6399
3. Run fr_benchmark against it with standard workloads:
   - SET only: 100k requests, 50 clients, pipeline 1
   - GET only: 100k requests, 50 clients, pipeline 1 (pre-populate keys first)
   - Mixed 50/50 SET/GET: 100k requests, 50 clients
   - Pipeline depth 16: 100k requests, 50 clients
   - INCR: 100k requests, 50 clients
4. Stop frankenredis, start legacy redis-server on same port
5. Run identical workloads against legacy Redis
6. Record all results as JSON in baselines/:
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_set.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_get.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_mixed.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_pipeline16.json
   - baselines/frankenredis_v0.1.0_incr.json
   - baselines/redis_7.2_set.json (comparison anchor)
   - baselines/redis_7.2_get.json
   - etc.
7. Create scripts/record_baselines.sh that automates steps 1-6
8. Create scripts/compare_baselines.py that reads two baseline files and reports deltas

## Validation
- baselines/ contains at least 10 JSON files (5 workloads x 2 servers)
- Each JSON has: ops_sec, p50_us, p95_us, p99_us, p999_us, total_time_sec, total_requests
- FrankenRedis throughput is at least 50% of legacy Redis for basic SET/GET (single-threaded, so parity is expected)
- If FrankenRedis is slower than 50% of Redis, create a follow-up bead to investigate

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (bxlo) — CLOSED, already implemented

## Files
- baselines/*.json (new, many files)
- scripts/record_baselines.sh (new)
- scripts/compare_baselines.py (new)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.143539757Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:52.009288902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:52.009108591Z","close_reason":"Recorded initial baselines, added automation scripts, and filed frankenredis-zjii for severe perf gap","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-gess","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-bxlo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.601521835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Match upstream error strings and arity validation for invalid/no-key commands, use real key-spec extraction instead of coarse first/last/step for special commands, and return per-key access flags that match Redis semantics. Add conformance coverage in core_server and targeted runtime tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:29:35.366382963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T16:52:10.476730250Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T16:52:10.474421609Z","close_reason":"Implemented upstream COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS parity and verified against live Redis","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ghz","title":"Convert NUM_DATABASES fixed arrays to Vec for runtime database count","description":"## Problem

Store uses `[usize; NUM_DATABASES]` fixed arrays for db_key_counts and db_expires_counts. NUM_DATABASES=16 is a compile-time const. Redis allows configuring database count at startup.

## Implementation Plan

1. Remove `pub const NUM_DATABASES: usize = 16` from fr-store
2. Add `database_count: usize` field to Store, defaulting to 16
3. Change db_key_counts and db_expires_counts to Vec<usize>
4. Initialize both Vecs in Store::new() with `vec![0; 16]`
5. Update all array access to use bounds-checked Vec indexing
6. In SELECT command: validate db < store.database_count (currently checks < NUM_DATABASES)
7. In encode_db_key/decode_db_key: validate db < database_count
8. In SWAPDB: validate both db indices
9. Accept database count as constructor parameter for Store

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: Major — all array references
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: SELECT validation, Store construction
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: May need to pass database_count at startup

## Testing

- Verify all existing tests pass (should be transparent since default stays 16)
- Add test: Store with 32 databases, SELECT 31 works, SELECT 32 fails
- Add test: CONFIG GET databases returns configured count","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:38.048658425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:33:47.868166696Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:33:47.867568142Z","close_reason":"db_key_counts/db_expires_counts converted from fixed [usize;16] arrays to Vec<usize>. database_count field added to Store. All references updated to use self.database_count.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-ghz","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hna","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:41.755004557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
